title: Dog Maggots Visible in Ear Wound: Emergency Triage Guide
meta_desc: If you see maggots in your dog's ear wound, it is a medical emergency. Learn why immediate veterinary care is critical and how to help your pet.
content_html:
What is it? (Myiasis)

Finding maggots in your dog's ear is a condition known as myiasis, often referred to as fly strike. This occurs when flies are attracted to an existing wound, moisture, or infection, and lay eggs that hatch into larvae. These larvae feed on the decaying or living tissue within the ear canal or wound site, which can lead to severe tissue destruction and secondary infections.

Is it an Emergency?

Yes. The urgency level is High. While it is distressing to see, it is vital to remain calm for your pet. Myiasis is considered a medical emergency because maggots can migrate deeper into the ear canal or into the bloodstream, potentially causing systemic infection, shock, or permanent damage to the ear structure. You should contact an emergency veterinarian immediately for professional removal and treatment.

How a Photo Helps Triage

If your dog is stable enough to allow it, taking a clear, well-lit photo of the affected ear can be incredibly helpful for the veterinary team. A photo allows the triage nurse to assess the extent of the infestation and the condition of the surrounding tissue before you even arrive. This helps the clinic prepare the necessary tools and medications, ensuring your dog receives the fastest possible care once you reach the hospital.

faqs.question: Can I remove the maggots myself at home?
faqs.answer: It is strongly discouraged. Attempting to remove maggots yourself can cause them to burrow deeper or leave mouthparts behind, which can lead to severe infection. Professional sedation and specialized tools are often required to ensure every larva is safely removed.

faqs.question: How did my dog get maggots in their ear?
faqs.answer: Flies are attracted to the smell of blood, pus, or moisture. If your dog had a small scratch, an ear infection with discharge, or even just wax buildup, a fly may have laid eggs in the area, which then hatched into maggots within hours.

faqs.question: Is myiasis painful for my dog?
faqs.answer: Yes, this condition is very painful and itchy. Your dog may be shaking their head, pawing at the ear, or acting lethargic. Prompt veterinary care includes pain management to keep your pet comfortable during recovery.
